name: Espubike Section 4: Las Alquerías - El Berro
name_original: Mirador de La Cabaña, Cueva de Los Mataos, Prado Mayor – Ruta Circular –  Sierra Espuña
description: Espubike Section 4 is a difficult 26.5-mile mountain biking route in Sierra Espuña Regional Park, featuring significant elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around National Park Sierra Espuña offers diverse and challenging terrain across a varied landscape. The region features pine forests, dramatic "badlands" with gullies and canyons, and peaks reaching up to 1,583 meters, such as Morrón de Espuña. Its uneven relief includes imposing features like the Paredes de Leyva cliff faces and the Ravines of Gebas, providing a range of environments for mountain bike trails. The park's average altitude is around 800 meters, contributing to significant elevation changes on many routes. Best mountain bike trails around National Park Sierra Espuña The most popular mountain bike trail is Prado Mayor, Collado del Humero, Collado Bermejo – Ruta Circular – Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña, a 23.0 miles (37.1 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete, featuring substantial elevation gain across varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Espubike Section 4: Las Alquerías - El Berro, a difficult 26.4 miles (42.6 km) path that traverses scenic areas including pine forests and villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Leiva Valley Trail – Cueva de los Mataos loop from Cabezo del Sopalmo, a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail leading through the Leiva Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around National Park Sierra Espuña is defined by pine forests, "badlands" landscapes, and significant mountain peaks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging ascents to routes through diverse natural features. The routes in National Park Sierra Espuña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra Espuña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: There are 15 mountain bike trails available in National Park Sierra Espuña on komoot. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from challenging ascents to scenic rides through varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Sierra Espuña?
A: National Park Sierra Espuña offers a diverse and challenging terrain. You'll encounter everything from dense pine forests and dramatic 'badlands' with gullies and canyons, to significant mountain peaks like Morrón de Espuña, which reaches up to 1,583 meters. The average altitude is around 800 meters, ensuring routes often feature substantial elevation changes.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Sierra Espuña?
A: While many trails in Sierra Espuña are rated as difficult, there is at least one easy route available for mountain bikers. The park's varied network also includes moderate options, allowing riders to find trails that match their skill level as they gain experience.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The park is rich in striking natural features and viewpoints. You can encounter the imposing Paredes de Leyva cliff faces, the unique 'lunar landscape' of the Ravines of Gebas, and historical Pozos de Nieve (snow pits). For panoramic views, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Airplane Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offer extensive vistas of the park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in National Park Sierra Espuña are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930101336/prado-mayor-collado-del-humero-collado-bermejo-ruta-circular-parque-regional-de-sierra-espuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prado Mayor, Collado del Humero, Collado Bermejo – Ruta Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.1 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40396210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Airplane Viewpoint – Collado Bermejo loop from Moriana&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 32.1 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: The mountain bike trails in National Park Sierra Espuña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning natural landscapes, and the rewarding ascents that lead to breathtaking views.

Q: Is the Espubike route part of the trails in Sierra Espuña?
A: Yes, the Espubike is a prominent circular route that traverses scenic areas of Sierra Espuña. You can experience a section of it with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921582909/espubike-section-4-las-alquerias-el-berro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espubike Section 4: Las Alquerías - El Berro&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 42.6 km path that takes you through pine forests and villages.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, National Park Sierra Espuña offers several long-distance trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14627877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Húmero – Collado Bermejo loop from Piedra del Campanario&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.3 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial ride through the park's varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Sierra Espuña?
A: National Park Sierra Espuña is home to diverse fauna, including 185 recorded species. You might spot the endemic Espuña squirrel, mouflons, and wild boars. The park is also a significant habitat for birds of prey such as eagle owls, peregrine falcons, goshawks, and golden eagles.

Q: Are there any trails that go through the Leiva Valley?
A: Yes, you can explore the Leiva Valley on a mountain bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiva Valley Trail – Cueva de los Mataos loop from Cabezo del Sopalmo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.7 km trail that leads through this scenic valley, offering a rewarding ride through its unique landscape.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Sierra Espuña?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the diverse terrain and Mediterranean climate suggest that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.
tips: Amazing views from a high viewpoint at Sierra Espuña – many mtb trails down from here.

Hidden between the peaks of Sierra Espuña, Prado Mayor is a tiny valley that you can descend from Collado Blanco (1,223 meters), at the head of the Leiva Valley. It offers good views and the gift of absolute silence.

This cave has a small maze of galleries. The cavity is not difficult or dangerous and the path to it is completely flat. It is very close to the recreational area of Casa Leyva. The flashlight of the mobile is enough to explore it.
If you go to Sierra Espuña you have to visit this viewpoint to enjoy its wonderful views.

faqs.question: What makes this mountain biking trail so challenging?
faqs.answer: This section is officially rated as 'difficult' due to its significant length of approximately 53 kilometers and a substantial accumulated ascent of around 1,270 meters. It requires a good level of fitness and is considered the most demanding part of the Espubike Challenge.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: On average, mountain bikers can expect to spend around 4 hours and 24 minutes to complete this route. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: This section runs point-to-point from Las Alquerías to El Berro. You would typically start in Las Alquerías. For parking, look for available spots in or near the village. As part of the larger Espubike Challenge, services like luggage transport are available, which can help with logistics for point-to-point sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse a variety of paths and trails, including the Algarrobo, Campix, Paredes del Leyva, and Barranco de la Hoz. The Sierra Espuña is known for its rugged, pine-covered mountains and deep ravines, offering a mix of challenging climbs and descents, with some lush, green areas where water is present.

faqs.question: What natural landmarks and viewpoints will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by notable natural landmarks such as Fuente del Hilo, La Perdiz, the dinosaur trail's hanging bridge, Fuente Blanca, and Prado Mayor. You can also enjoy stunning Mountain Views and visit the Cueva de los Mataos and the Cabin Viewpoint (Mirador de la Cabaña).

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, 62% of it follows the 'Espubike. Tramo 4 Las Alquerías - El Berro' trail, 42% is part of the 'Camino de la Cruz del Bajo Guadalentín', and 41% overlaps with 'Transmurciana 19'. You'll also find shorter segments intersecting with 'Esputrek. Tramo 3 La Perdiz - Las Alquerías' and 'Sendero Valle de Leiva - Collado Mangueta'.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Sierra Espuña Regional Park, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging nature of this mountain biking trail and the presence of wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, difficult ride and bring plenty of water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Espuña Regional Park can experience hot summers. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Winter can also be suitable, but be prepared for cooler conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to access the Sierra Espuña Regional Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access Sierra Espuña Regional Park itself. However, for specific activities or if you're participating in organized events like the full Espubike Challenge, there might be associated fees or registration requirements. Always check local regulations before your visit, especially for large groups or commercial activities.

faqs.question: Are there any services available for mountain bikers undertaking the Espubike Challenge?
faqs.answer: Yes, for those tackling the full Espubike Challenge, which this section is a part of, various services are available. These can include GPS and bike rental, guides, luggage transport between stages, technical assistance, and accommodation booking assistance. These services can be particularly helpful for a multi-day, challenging route like the Espubike.

waypoints.name: Cabin Viewpoint (Mirador de la Cabaña), Sierra Espuña Regional Park
